$comment: Canonical JLCPCB fabrication design-rule reference — the single source for the daemon's DRC-rule fallback baseline (internal/app/pcb_rules.go defaultPcbRules) and for design-flow DFM checks. JLCPCB (嘉立创) is the fab behind EasyEDA Pro / 嘉立创EDA, so a live board's `pcb.drc.rules` (getCurrentRuleConfiguration) SHOULD converge with the 'recommended' values here. ALWAYS prefer the live rule when connected; use this only as the seed when the live rule is missing or too loose.
source: JLCPCB published PCB capabilities — https://jlcpcb.com/capabilities/pcb-capabilities (+ jlcpcb.com/blog design guides)
units: each value carries mil and mm; process assumed 1oz outer copper, standard (non-surcharge) tier unless noted

convergenceCheck.verdict: ceshi's live pcb.drc.rules matches this reference's 'recommended' column (clear 6 / width 10 / min 5 / via 0.3-0.6) — the JLCEDA default DRC config reflects JLCPCB capabilities, so read-live and this-fallback agree.

manufacturingMin.$comment: ABSOLUTE floor for the standard tier — going below costs more or is rejected. Use these as CLAMP floors (never emit a track/via/gap below the applicable min).

traceWidthMil.note: 3.5mil only in BGA/multilayer; 4mil possible at surcharge

copperToEdgeMil.note: board-outline-to-copper clearance = the pour pull-back / inset floor

recommended.$comment: Reliable defaults for AUTO-GENERATED designs — what defaultPcbRules() should seed when the live rule is unavailable. Chosen well above the manufacturing floor for yield.
recommended.$viaNote: via size depends on layer count — see byLayerCount

byLayerCount.$comment: Recommended via sizes + tightest trace/space by stackup (JLCPCB blog guidance).

4.note: 4L is same base price as 2L at JLCPCB in many promos; inner planes de-densify the surface

byCopperWeight.$comment: Heavier copper needs wider min trace/space (etch factor).

impedanceNote: Controlled impedance (Z0) is NOT derivable from platform data (stackup Er / dielectric height / copper weight are not readable via eda.*). JLCPCB publishes stackup tables + an impedance calculator; a controlled-Z design must supply stackup + target Z0, then compute trace width externally (Wheeler/IPC-2141) — see task #27. This reference intentionally omits Z0 widths.
